urgency.message: 93 critical CVE(s) and 7 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Meta Contractors Posed as Teens to Prompt Rival Chatbots About Suicide, Sex, and Drugs
items.summary: Hundreds of contractors working on a project for Meta pretended to be kids in order to see how other chatbots like Gemini and ChatGPT would respond to high-risk subjects, WIRED found.

items.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
items.summary: The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.

items.title: Vulnerabilities Expose Private Data in Indian Government Systems
items.summary: One critical vulnerability, among many discovered by a researcher, could have allowed anyone to walk in and take over a national government portal.

items.title: Nissan discloses employee data breach linked to Oracle zero-day attacks
items.summary: Nissan is warning that it suffered a data breach affecting current and former employees after threat actors exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft vulnerability in data theft attacks previously linked to the ShinyHunters extortion group. [...]

items.title: NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
items.summary: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) says the ShinyHunters extortion group stole only publicly available data, outdated logs, and configuration files after breaching its systems by exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in an Oracle PeopleSoft server. [...]

items.title: Can Clothes Make You Invisible to Facial Recognition?
items.summary: Does life feel Orwellian sometimes? One researcher has a solution for you: graphic tees that confuse the neural networks in surveillance cameras.

items.title: Iran, Russia, China Target Water Systems for Sabotage
items.summary: Nation-state attackers breach water systems through weak passwords, exposed PLCs, and poor segmentation — not sophisticated malware.

items.title: Justices rule that cellphone location histories are protected by the Fourth Amendment
items.summary: Police must get a warrant to request geofence data involving individual cellphones, the U.S. Supreme Court ruled in what represents a victory for privacy advocates.

items.title: Malicious Perplexity Chrome Extension Intercepted Searches and Address Bar Input
items.summary:
Microsoft has found a malicious Chrome extension that posed as the AI search engine Perplexity and quietly logged what people searched for. It routed every query and every character typed into the address bar through an attacker-controlled server before redirecting users to real results.

Microsoft

items.title: WhatsApp rolls out usernames to help users hide their phone number
items.summary: WhatsApp is finally allowing users to reserve usernames, a privacy feature that lets them hide their phone numbers from people not in their contact list. [...]

items.title: Microsoft extends Windows Server 2022 hotpatching until October 2027
items.summary: Microsoft has extended Windows Server 2022 hotpatching until October 2027, one year after the mainstream end date of October 2026. [...]

items.title: US racks up about 400 wins over illegal World Cup streaming sites
items.summary: The World Cup’s organizing body, FIFA, helped identify hundreds of  domains taken down in an action organized by the U.S., along with the help of U.S. broadcaster NBC Universal and other entities.

items.title: WhatsApp is Finally Getting Usernames to Help Keep Phone Numbers Private
items.summary:
WhatsApp on Monday officially announced the start of global reservations of usernames with an aim to protect the privacy of more than three billion users on the messaging platform.

The optional feature is designed to help users connect with someone on the service through usernames, as opposed to di

items.title: WhatsApp Rolling Out Username Feature to Bolster Phone Number Privacy
items.summary:
An optional ‘username key’ adds another layer by requiring a secondary credential before someone can message users.
The post WhatsApp Rolling Out Username Feature to Bolster Phone Number Privacy appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: US posts $10 million reward over Russian cyber campaign targeting Signal, WhatsApp
items.summary: Russia-linked hacking groups tracked as UNC5792 and UNC4221 have socially engineered their way into the messaging accounts of government officials.

items.title: U.S. offers $10 million for hackers targeting WhatsApp, Signal users
items.summary: The U.S. Department of State is offering up to $10 million for information that helps identify or locate members of the UNC5792 and UNC4221 hacker groups, which are linked to Russia's intelligence and military services. [...]

items.title: Mustang Panda Uses Zoho WorkDrive as Command Channel in Indian Government Attacks
items.summary:
The China-aligned espionage group Mustang Panda is running two campaigns against the Indian government and hydropower targets, deploying new malware and turning a legitimate cloud service into its command channel.

Acronis Threat Research Unit found active compromises inside Indian go

items.title: OpenAI Reveals GPT-5.6 Sol Cybersecurity Model, Restricts Early Access
items.summary: OpenAI is previewing its GPT-5.6 Sol model to a vetted few at the US government's request

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Kernel Flaws, AI Malware Tricks, Turla Backdoor, Infostealers and More
items.summary:
This week was a reminder that attackers do not always need big tricks. One small mistake, one old access path, one missed patch, and suddenly the door is open.

The noise is not all noise, either. Forums are talking, researchers are finding easy cracks, and defenders have more cleanup waiting.

Here

items.title: Telegram-Based Millenium RAT Campaign Infects 60,000 Devices
items.summary: Group-IB says Millenium RAT, now rewritten in C++, has hit 62,289 devices in 160+ countries

items.title: Researchers Demo New Claude Code Attack Using Harmless-Looking Repositories to Hijack Developer Machines
items.summary:
Indirect prompts hidden in a repository can lead to Claude Code spawning a reverse shell on the developer’s machine.
The post Researchers Demo New Claude Code Attack Using Harmless-Looking Repositories to Hijack Developer Machines appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Straiker Raises $64 Million for AI Security Platform
items.summary:
The startup’s platform can identify AI agents and provide visibility into their access, behavior, and risks.
The post Straiker Raises $64 Million for AI Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: 29th June – Threat Intelligence Report
items.summary: For the latest discoveries in cyber research for the week of 29th June, please download our Threat Intelligence Bulletin. TOP ATTACKS AND BREACHES Polymarket, a large cryptocurrency-based prediction market, has confirmed a supply chain attack after a third-party frontend vendor breach led to malicio

items.title: Agentic AI Has an Identity Problem and Attackers Know It
items.summary: AI agents can access data, trigger workflows, and take action across enterprise systems. Token Security explains why governing these privileged identities is becoming essential for enterprise security. [...]

items.title: Critical SimpleHelp flaw exploited to deploy new stealer malware
items.summary: Hackers are exploiting a recently disclosed critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-48558) in SimpleHelp to deploy Djinn Stealer, a previously undocumented cross-platform information stealer targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux. [...]

items.title: Hackers now exploit critical Oracle E-Business flaw in attacks
items.summary: Attackers have begun exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-46817) in the Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) financial application, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]

items.title: Insurance Regulators Group NAIC Hit in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack
items.summary:
The ShinyHunters extortion group claims to have stolen 3.1 TB of data from the organization.
The post Insurance Regulators Group NAIC Hit in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Ukraine to use seized crypto from cybercrime group to buy war bonds
items.summary: Ukraine's Asset Recovery and Management Agency (ARMA), which manages property seized in criminal proceedings, said more than $8.3 million in cryptocurrency had been transferred to its official digital wallet following a court order.

items.title: 236,000 DCloud Uni-App Sites Used in Crypto Scams, Phishing, and Wallet Drainers
items.summary:
New findings unearthed by Infoblox show that more than 236,000 websites are using investment scam templates built using a legitimate Chinese open-source, cross-platform application development framework called DCloud Uni-App.

The templates power bogus cryptocurrency exchanges, multi-language pig-bu

items.title: Amazon Q VS Extension Flaw Leads to Cloud Credential Theft
items.summary: Adversaries could plant a malicious repository that can execute arbitrary code and steal cloud credentials by exploiting the vulnerability, which showcases growing MCP risk.

items.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
items.summary: Today’s encrypted data, such as credentials, may no longer remain confidential in the future because the public-key cryptography protecting it will soon be broken by quantum computers. Although no machine today can break elliptic curve cryptography or RSA, quantum hardware is advancing rapidly and w

items.title: Gamaredon Expands Ukraine Attacks with New Malware and Cloud Service Abuse
items.summary:
A Russian advanced persistent threat (APT) group has continued to evolve and expand its malware arsenal as part of its ongoing cyber onslaught against Ukraine throughout 2025.

Slovakian cybersecurity company ESET said it observed 35 distinct spear-phishing campaigns mounted by Gamaredon against new

items.title: ‘DirtyClone’ Linux Kernel Vulnerability Leads to Root Access
items.summary:
A variant of DirtyFrag, the flaw allows unprivileged local users to manipulate the Linux page cache and gain root privileges.
The post ‘DirtyClone’ Linux Kernel Vulnerability Leads to Root Access appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: OpenAI and Anthropic Limit New AI Models to Trump-Approved Customers During Cybersecurity Review
items.summary:
ChatGPT maker OpenAI said Friday it is restricting the release of its new artificial intelligence model at the request of President Donald Trump’s administration.
The post OpenAI and Anthropic Limit New AI Models to Trump-Approved Customers During Cybersecurity Review appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Top Google Security Staff Warn Search Data Could Be Hacked if EU Rules Change
items.summary: Europe’s pro-competition proposals could see Google Search and Android systems opened up. The company claims there are serious privacy flaws.

items.title: US Federal Insurance Regulator Confirms Data Breach Via Oracle Flaw
items.summary: An attacker has exploited a zero day in Oracle Peoplesoft to gain access to the IT systems of the NAIC, the standard-setting association for the US federal insurance system

items.title: US Offers $10 Million Bounty for Russian State Hackers as Messaging App Attacks Evolve
items.summary:
UNC5792 and UNC4221 have been targeting US government officials, military leaders, and allied personnel.
The post US Offers $10 Million Bounty for Russian State Hackers as Messaging App Attacks Evolve appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Russian Hackers Accused of Destructive Cyber-Attack on Jaguar Land Rover
items.summary: Experts warn the Jaguar Land Rover breach bears hallmarks of Kremlin-backed hackers, citing novel ransomware, strategic timing and efforts to obscure attribution

items.title: Microsoft Removes 119 Edge Extensions That Hid Malware in Images and Fonts
items.summary:
Microsoft has shut down a long-running malicious extension operation on the Edge Add-ons store that hid its payloads inside ordinary image and font files, then woke up days after install to steal credentials and run ad fraud.

The company calls it StegoAd, a mash-up of steganography and adware,

items.title: FBI Sounds Alarm Over Russian Intelligence Signal Phishing
items.summary: The FBI claims Russian spies are targeting Signal backup keys

items.summary:
The company says Sol matches competing systems like Mythos Preview while using only a third of the output tokens.

items.title: CVE-2023-6606 Kernel: out-of-bounds read vulnerability in smbcalcsize
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk
items.summary: Rising threats from third-party actors are forcing institutions to play defense to protect student data from ransomware and other attacks.
